results.title: Addressing Root Cause of Child's Aggressive Behaviour When Faced with Child's Uncooperative Family
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the specific measures to address the root cause of a child's aggressive behaviour in bullying cases involving uncooperative families were already addressed via a written reply on 14 October 2025. Members are directed to refer to the response to Question No 46 in the 14 October Order Paper for the full details. No new operational constraints, timelines, or additional interventions were announced during this oral session.

results.title: Activation Triggers when Minors are Reported Missing or at Risk of Self-harm
results.summary: The Police respond to reports of missing minors or those at risk of self-harm by arriving at the scene within 15 minutes, with the Singapore Civil Defence Force activated for medical and operational support. Technology deployment includes reviewing available closed-circuit television footage and utilizing drones to identify likely locations, while online appeals are issued for information when family consent is granted. Frontline officers receive trauma-informed interaction training to build trust and ensure safety when dealing with vulnerable persons, covering suicide intervention and trauma management. The summary confirms these specific operational protocols and training components without inferring broader policy implications.

results.title: Accessibility Features in Autonomous Shuttle Services
results.summary: During the initial pilot phase of autonomous shuttle services, safety operators and ground ambassadors will assist passengers, while only larger eight-seater vehicles can currently accommodate folded wheelchairs and strollers. The Ministry of Transport acknowledges that current five-seater pilot vehicles do not fully meet diverse accessibility needs due to market constraints and plans to share feedback with manufacturers to incorporate universal design features from the onset. Moving forward, the agency intends to adopt universal design principles for infrastructure at pick-up and drop-off points as the fleet scales up, aiming to avoid costly retrofits and better serve commuters with mobility needs, families with strollers, and others.

results.title: Additional Safety Measures to Prevent Maritime Collisions
results.summary: Following the 14 June 2024 collision between Vox Maxima and Marine Honour, the Maritime and Port Authority of Singapore (MPA) has implemented immediate safety measures directed at the vessel's managing company. These actions include prosecuting four crew members under the Merchant Shipping Act 1995, updating the "Vessel's Checklist for Piloted Movements" to mandate emergency anchor release readiness, and integrating collision scenarios into harbour pilot simulator training. MPA also maintains existing emergency protocols for power loss incidents. Long-term, the authority is studying the feasibility of stationing harbour tugs at critical port areas to support vessels facing mechanical or electrical failures. All six safety recommendations from the Transport Safety Investigation Bureau have been issued to the relevant operator.

results.title: Addressing Under-reporting of Scam Cases and Measures to Encourage Victims to File Reports
results.summary: The Ministry lacks estimates on the extent of scam case under-reporting in Singapore. To encourage reporting, the "I can ACT (Add, Check, Tell)" national anti-scam campaign advises the public to lodge Police reports regardless of monetary loss. This guidance is included in resource guides disseminated to all households by the Police starting July 2025. No specific measures beyond this advisory campaign have been stated to address under-reporting.

results.title: Aligning Commercial Interests of Insurance Companies with Needs of Doctors and Patients
results.summary: The Minister for Health stated that experimental treatments, lacking rigorous assessment for safety, efficacy, and cost-effectiveness, are ineligible for mainstream financing or insurance schemes. Consequently, these treatments are supported exclusively through research grants and extended only to selected patients rather than being covered for all insured individuals. This policy ensures that commercial interests do not override the requirement for proven clinical value before public funds are utilized for such interventions.

results.title: Aligning Penalties for Vaping Substance Impaired Driving with Drink-driving Traffic Offences and Strengthening Deterrent Sentencing Measures
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for National Security confirmed that penalties for driving under the influence of drugs, including vaping substances like etomidate, are currently aligned with drink-driving offences, carrying the same penalties plus additional sanctions for drug consumption. The Minister stated that penalties are reviewed periodically and affirmed that suggestions for stronger deterrent measures will be considered in due course. No specific timeline or new legislative changes were announced at this stage.

results.title: Allocating Students to Junior College Courses Before Knowing if They Qualify under Joint Admissions Exercise
results.summary: Under the Joint Admission Exercise, students are posted to a Junior College course only if they meet the respective cut-off points for their selected Arts or Science stream. The Minister clarified that allocation occurs without prior qualification checks, as eligibility is confirmed upon posting. In isolated instances where a student faces significant difficulty coping with specific subjects, the JC may advise a different subject combination or transfer to another course. One such case occurred in 2025, where a student was not offered a specific subject combination within their posted course.

results.title: Annual Applicant Rejection Rates for Government Mental Health Support Networks and Reasons for Rejection
results.summary: Government-affiliated mental health support networks, including CREST, COMIT, CREST-Youth, and YIT, do not reject walk-in or referred applicants. Instead, they assess needs and refer individuals to other services when appropriate. In 2024, 316 clients were referred to hospitals or other services due to severe mental health conditions, the need for specialised care, or client preference to change services. The Minister clarified that rejections do not occur within these teams, as referrals are made based on clinical necessity or client choice rather than denial of support.

results.title: Assessing Current State of Racial and Religious Relations in Singapore
results.summary: The Government assesses racial and religious relations in Singapore as generally healthy, though it remains vigilant against external influences like the Israel-Hamas conflict and US political violence that could normalize divisive discourse. To prevent polarization and social fissures, the Government actively manages public discussions and leverages existing legal frameworks, including the Penal Code, Maintenance of Religious Harmony Act, and Foreign Interference (Countermeasures) Act. Operational measures include the National Steering Committee on Racial and Religious Harmony, which convenes apex religious leaders, and the Racial and Religious Harmony Circles, which facilitate cross-cultural dialogues and inter-faith initiatives across constituencies to build trust and understanding.

results.title: Assessing Feasibility of National Toy and Board Game Lending Service and Integration into NLB Network
results.summary: The Minister for Digital Development and Information stated that the National Library Board (NLB) has no plans to launch a national toy and board-game lending service or integrate such a system into its network. While the Punggol Toy Library pilot aims to foster community connections, NLB's current operational focus remains strictly on promoting reading and literacy through existing lending and book delivery services. Consequently, the Ministry will not assess public demand for toy lending or conduct feasibility studies for this specific initiative, as it falls outside the library's core mandate.

results.title: Assessment of Non-resident Population Increase from 2021 to 2025 and Contributing Factors
results.summary: Work Permit Holders accounted for 60% of the non-resident population increase between 2021 and 2025, driven by construction sector needs for public housing and infrastructure like Changi Terminal 5, while migrant domestic workers contributed 13% to meet caregiving demand. Both groups are transient. The apparent 30% rise reflects a lower 2021 base caused by pandemic border restrictions and economic weakness; as restrictions eased, firms backfilled positions, resulting in an average annual increase of 6.8% from 2021 to 2025 versus 2.2% against the 2019 pre-pandemic baseline. The Minister clarified that this growth is temporary and contextualized by the recovery from COVID-19 constraints.

results.title: Baseline Categories of Stalls in Socially-conscious Enterprise Hawker Centres
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment stated that Socially-conscious Enterprise Hawker Centre (SEHC) operators currently curate stall mixes based on patron needs and demands. The Ministry will consider the suggestion to mandate baseline categories, such as economy rice stalls, in these centres. No specific timeline or eligibility criteria for this consideration were provided in the response.

results.title: Block-wide Fire Alarms to Provide Early Warning for Fires in HDB Flats
results.summary: The Ministry for Home Affairs declined to implement block-wide fire alarms in HDB flats, maintaining reliance on individual Household Fire Alarm Devices (HFADs). The Coordinating Minister explained that block-wide evacuations are seldom required, as HDB flats are designed as fire compartments where only affected floors and two above are typically evacuated by the Singapore Civil Defence Force. Additionally, common activities like cooking could trigger false alarms, potentially driving residents to disconnect HFADs or disregard warnings. Consequently, the current strategy focuses on individual unit alerts rather than centralised systems to prevent dangerous resident behaviours.

results.title: Breakdown of ABSD Collected from PRs by Property Type and Price Range over Last Five Years
results.summary: From 2020 to 2024, the average annual number of residential transactions involving Additional Buyer's Stamp Duty (ABSD) levied on Singapore Permanent Residents (SPRs) was approximately 6,600, with corresponding average collections of S$462 million. By transaction volume, SPR purchases were distributed as follows: 33% for HDB properties, 43% for private properties under S$1.5 million, and 24% for private properties at or above S$1.5 million. In terms of ABSD collected, the distribution shifted to 12% for HDB properties, 31% for private properties under S$1.5 million, and 57% for private properties at or above S$1.5 million. Joint purchases by SPRs and foreigners are excluded from these figures as they are taxed under the higher foreigner ABSD rate.

results.title: Breakdown of Employment Pass Holders and New EPs Issued by Years of Working Experience over Last 10 Years
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower provided data on Employment Pass (EP) holders and new EPs issued over the last 10 years, categorized by years of working experience in five-year bands. These figures, presented in Tables 1 and 2, reflect experience declared by employers during EP applications, covering both Singapore and overseas work history. The response details the specific breakdown requested but does not include analysis of trends or future policy directions.

results.title: Breakdown of Healthcare Research Funding Allocation under RIE by Disease Area, Research Theme and Programme Type
results.summary: The Ministry of Health publishes annual healthcare research funding allocations by programme type on its website. However, the Ministry does not release detailed breakdowns of these allocations by disease area or research theme. The $2.4 billion committed under the national Research, Innovation and Enterprise plans is managed without granular disclosure for specific disease categories or thematic research areas.

results.title: Breakdown of New Citizenships and Permanent Residencies by Country of Birth for Insights into Singapore's Population Trends
results.summary: The Government will not release breakdowns of new citizenships and permanent residencies by country of birth, maintaining its previous stance. This position remains unchanged as disclosing such data could create bilateral sensitivities and is not deemed to be in the national interest. Consequently, more granular data for citizens to understand societal trends will not be provided. The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs confirmed that this policy constraint applies to all future data releases on this specific metric.

results.title: Breakdown on Preschools Using In-house Cooks, Ready-to-eat Meals and Catered Hot Food
results.summary: The Early Childhood Development Agency does not track the specific breakdown of preschools using in-house cooks, ready-to-eat meals, or catered hot food. Instead, all preschools must adhere to hygiene requirements under the Early Childhood Development Centres Regulations and Code of Practice. These mandatory standards ensure food premises remain clean and pest-free while implementing proper food storage procedures, regardless of the chosen food provision arrangement.

results.title: Broadening Scope of Sovereign Green Bond Issuances Beyond Financing Nationally Significant Infrastructure
results.summary: The Government confirmed no plans to broaden sovereign green bond issuances beyond nationally significant infrastructure like the Cross Island and Jurong Region Lines. Under the Significant Infrastructure Government Loan Act (SINGA), sovereign borrowing is restricted to major, long-term infrastructure projects that comply with both SINGA and the Singapore Green Bond Framework. While Statutory Boards such as the HDB, NEA, and PUB issue separate green bonds for green buildings and sustainable utilities, the sovereign instrument remains limited to qualifying infrastructure. Consequently, the updated Singapore Green Bond Framework and Singapore-Asia Taxonomy currently do not expand the scope for sovereign issuances to other project categories.

results.title: CDC Vouchers Eligibility for Singaporean Citizens Staying at Nursing Homes
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth confirmed that every Singaporean household may claim only one set of Community Development Council (CDC) vouchers per tranche. Residents in nursing homes sharing an institutional address are not automatically excluded; instead, the CDCs are collaborating with the Ministry of Health to facilitate claims by obtaining necessary documentation from the homes for appeals at Community Centres or Clubs. The government will continue working with relevant Ministries to assist eligible residents in accessing these benefits and support measures.

results.title: Citizenship Demographics of Hired Bus Captains and Drivers
results.summary: From 2015 to 2024, the total number of bus captains increased by approximately 9%, rising from 8,900 to 9,700. As of December 2024, the nationality breakdown for bus captains is 28% Singaporeans, 15% Permanent Residents, and 57% foreign Work Pass holders. The proportion of foreign captains remained largely stable throughout the period, except for a temporary dip during the COVID-19 pandemic. The Acting Minister for Transport provided these figures in response to a parliamentary question regarding citizenship demographics in the public transport sector.

results.title: Clarification by Minister for Manpower
results.summary: Minister for Manpower Dr Tan See Leng clarified that the Deep Skills Programme (DPS) framework remains under review to balance sustainability, affordability, and sufficient payouts for members at age 65 or 70. Approximately 75% of lapsed DPS coverage involves members aged above 55 who are no longer working or were previously self-employed, lacking Ordinary Account contributions for premiums. Of those whose coverage lapsed by 31 December 2022, around 40% have reinstated or started new cover by 31 December 2024. Currently, over two million Singaporeans and Permanent Residents are covered under DPS, with 86% of active CPF members aged 21 to 65 participating. These figures contextualize lapse rates against the broader coverage base.

results.title: Clarification on Defining Section 126B(aa) of Employment Act 1968 towards Errant Employers
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower clarified that employers must submit a Mandatory Retrenchment Notification (MRN) within five working days of issuing a retrenchment notice. Administrative penalties under the Employment (Administrative Penalties) Regulations 2016 impose a $1,000 fine for the first contravention and $2,000 for subsequent failures to submit accurate or timely MRNs. While most breaches are treated as civil contraventions, continued non-compliance may lead to a criminal offence under section 126D if an employer ignores an authorised officer's direction, risking fines up to $5,000 or imprisonment of up to six months. Data indicates 70% of employers submitted MRNs on time between January 2021 and June 2025, suggesting high overall compliance despite the deterrent effect of these penalties.

results.title: Collisions between Cyclists and Pedestrians on Cycling Paths Adjacent to Pedestrian-only Paths
results.summary: Between 1 July 2025 and 31 August 2025, 20 accidents involving active mobility device users and pedestrians were reported, though specific collisions on cycling paths adjacent to pedestrian-only paths (POPs) are not tracked. Pedestrians are not legally prohibited from using these cycling paths to afford the majority of path users flexibility to walk when safe, while the conversion of adjacent footpaths to POPs aims to improve safety for those avoiding faster-moving cyclists. The Ministry's approach relies on clear path demarcation, strengthening user etiquette through education and outreach, and on-the-ground enforcement officers educating pedestrians to stay on POPs for their own safety. The Land Transport Authority will continue partnering with the community and schools to raise awareness on safe path use.

results.title: Combating Online Xenophobia Targeting New Citizens through Stronger Digital Regulations
results.summary: The Minister confirmed that hate speech and xenophobic abuse targeting new citizens are addressed under existing frameworks, including the Broadcasting Act, Penal Code, and amended Maintenance of Religious Harmony Act, which empower the Infocomm Media Development Authority to direct content removal and restrict access to harmful material. Criminal offences exist for acts promoting racial enmity or prejudicing racial harmony, with the Online Criminal Harms Act enabling access restrictions for online perpetuations. The Ministry of Digital Development and Information and the Ministry of Law will soon table the Online Safety (Relief and Accountability) Bill to establish a new Online Safety Commission by the first half of 2026. This dedicated agency will support victims of specified online harms, such as incitement of enmity or violence against any group, in seeking timely relief from perpetrators and platforms.

results.title: Common Complaints on Disamenities Caused by Foreign-registered Vehicles
results.summary: The most common complaints regarding foreign-registered vehicles concern illegal parking and pollutive emissions. The Land Transport Authority, National Environment Agency, and Traffic Police conduct regular joint enforcement operations to address these issues. Drivers failing emissions tests face immediate composition sums, while illegal parking penalties apply equally to all vehicles regardless of registration status. The public is encouraged to report feedback directly to the relevant agencies for action.

results.title: Complaints from Sentosa Residents on Poor Mobile Network Connectivity
results.summary: Over the past five years, the Infocomm Media Development Authority received four complaints regarding poor mobile network connectivity at two Sentosa condominiums. Operators have resolved the issue for one condominium and are currently enhancing coverage for the second. No further actions or timelines were specified for the remaining coverage enhancement efforts.

results.title: Consideration in Extending Electrical Wiring Checks under Home Improvement Scheme
results.summary: The Minister clarified that HDB's electrical wiring checks under the Home Improvement Programme and Enhancement for Active Seniors prioritise key components like circuit breakers and water heaters due to higher hazard risks, rather than covering general flat wiring. Fires may also stem from user behaviour such as socket overloading or uncertified devices. Residents with issues beyond HDB programme coverage must engage a Licensed Electrical Worker for assessment and rectification. For those needing financial assistance, HDB will refer them to the People's Association or social service agencies. The Energy Market Authority's website provides additional safety tips for residents.

results.title: Current Breakdown of Resident and Foreign Full-time Workers across Industry Sectors
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower publishes industry-specific breakdowns of resident and foreign full-time workers on its website. However, data distinguishing full-time from part-time status is available exclusively for resident workers, as such metrics are not collected for foreign workers. Consequently, the current public dataset does not provide a full-time/part-time split for the foreign workforce. Users seeking this specific granularity for foreign employees should note this operational constraint when interpreting available statistics.

results.title: Data Collection and Categorisation of Causes of Individual Bankruptcies in Last 10 Years
results.summary: The top five causes of individual bankruptcies over the past decade are business failure, overspending, loss of income, guarantee liabilities, and low income. This data is self-declared by debtors via a drop-down list in their Statement of Affairs. To foster responsible entrepreneurship, the Government offers startup support schemes like Accredited Mentor Partners and grants, while the Startup SG Founder grant mandates dollar-for-dollar matching to ensure financial stake. The Ministry of Law regularly reviews the bankruptcy regime to balance entrepreneurial risk-taking with financial responsibility. The Minister noted that entrepreneurs must make sound financing decisions to maintain a healthy entrepreneurial culture.

results.title: Data on Debts Repaid to Creditors of Bankruptcy Estates in Last 10 Years and Plans to Enhance Creditor Recovery
results.summary: The percentage of proven debts repaid in bankruptcy cases varies based on individual circumstances, whereas recovery rates under the Debt Repayment Scheme (DRS) are comparatively more favourable. The DRS applies to debtors with regular income and unsecured debts not exceeding $150,000, ensuring creditors receive no less than they would have in a standard bankruptcy while allowing debtors to avoid bankruptcy if obligations are met. The Ministry of Law maintains regular dialogues with creditors to ensure the viability of repayment plans under the DRS. While the Official Assignee makes reasonable efforts to assist in debt recovery, the Government balances creditor interests against allowing bankrupts to make a fresh start in their financial affairs.

results.title: Data on Employment of Local Graduates of IHLs and Aquaculture CET Programmes in Sea-based Fish Farm Industry
results.summary: The Singapore Food Agency does not collect data on the proportion of local graduates from Institutes of Higher Learning or Continuing Education and Training programmes employed in sea-based fish farms. Consequently, there is no available statistic regarding the employment rates of these graduates within farms receiving Agri-Food Cluster Transformation Fund support. The Ministry has confirmed the absence of this specific dataset, meaning no figures exist to answer the query on local graduate representation or fund recipient employment in this sector.

results.title: Data on Food Poisoning Cases in Preschools under Anchor and Partner Operator Schemes and Private Operators over Last Nine Months
results.summary: Over the last nine months, eight investigations into gastroenteritis outbreaks occurred in preschools, potentially viral or foodborne. The Early Childhood Development Agency does not track food poisoning cases by operational scheme (Anchor, Partner, or Private). All preschools must adhere to the Early Childhood Development Centres Regulations and Code of Practice, requiring disinfection of contaminated surfaces and monitoring of symptoms for suspected or known gastroenteritis cases to safeguard children's health and limit outbreak impact.

results.title: Data on Illegitimate Births and Subsequent Adoptions since 2020 and Destigmatisation Measures
results.summary: From 2020 to 2024, Singapore recorded an average of 745 non-marital live births annually to citizen mothers, with only 12 children subsequently adopted by their biological parents during this period. Under the Legitimacy Act 1934, such children are not considered legitimate unless parents marry or the child is adopted, though adoption social investigations typically take about nine months. The Government does not differentiate benefits for citizen children based on parental marital status, explicitly stating that single unwed parents do not need to adopt their own children to access subsidies for education, healthcare, childcare, or infant care. To address stigma, the Ministry of Social and Family Development integrates empathy and respect into the Character and Citizenship Education Curriculum while ensuring teachers support all children regardless of family background.

results.title: Data on Large and Foreign-registered Vehicles Speeding in Overtaking Lanes and Enforcement Measures against Speeding
results.summary: For 2024, the Traffic Police reported speeding violations by vehicle type but do not track specific lane usage or reasons for speeding. Offenders frequently cite urgency to reach appointments as a contributing factor. Foreign-registered vehicles with outstanding fines may be denied entry into Singapore by the Immigration and Checkpoints Authority until payments are made. The Ministry did not provide specific counts of foreign-registered vehicles caught speeding in right lanes, nor did it confirm whether targeted enforcement against such vehicles will be considered.

results.title: Data on Low Pay Incidence in Singapore as Compared to Other Countries per OECD's Definition
results.summary: Singapore's low-pay incidence, defined as the share of full-time residents earning less than two-thirds of median wages, stood at approximately 30% in 2024, significantly higher than the OECD average of 13% based on 2023 data. The Minister noted that this comparison does not account for Government transfers like Workfare, which support lower-wage workers in Singapore. Despite the higher incidence, real gross monthly income at the 20th percentile rose cumulatively by 6.3% from 2021 to 2024, outpacing the 3.2% rise in real median wages. Tripartite partners will continue efforts to uplift lower-wage workers through the Progressive Wage Model and National Wages Council recommendations to narrow the wage gap and support well-being in the coming years.

results.title: Data on MDWs Seeking Treatment for Mental Health Conditions
results.summary: Between 2022 and 2024, fewer than 20 migrant domestic workers (MDWs) sought mental health treatment at public healthcare institutions annually, with no recorded cases of legal issues during such access. Under the Employment of Foreign Manpower Act, employers must cover MDW medical expenses, including mental health treatments, and face fines up to $10,000, imprisonment for up to 12 months, or future employment bans for denying necessary care. Currently, MDWs must declare mental health conditions during pre-employment medical examinations; positive declarations or suspicions may trigger further assessments at additional costs. While some employers express concerns about MDW well-being, the Ministry advises seeking medical advice and referrals to mental health professionals rather than implementing mandatory assessments that could increase costs.

results.title: Data on Mental Health Crisis Intervention and Training for Frontline Police Officers and Post-incident Follow-up Support
results.summary: The Police aim to respond to suicide risk activations within 15 minutes using the nearest available resource, though specific activation numbers and age breakdowns are not tracked. All frontline officers receive general incident training plus a Mental Health Awareness Course developed by the Agency of Integrated Care to recognize symptoms and engage affected individuals. Additional specialized resources, including the Singapore Civil Defence Force's Disaster Assistance and Rescue Teams (DART) for complex rescues and the Crisis Negotiation Unit (CNU) with psychologists, are deployed when necessary. Established protocols refer cases to mental health professionals, such as the Institute of Mental Health's Crisis Response Team, for immediate assessment and potential conveyance to IMH for psychiatric support.

results.title: Data on Rejected Applications of LTVP and LTVP+ due to Criminal History, Conduct Issues or Income Requirements
results.summary: From 2020 to 2024, approximately 7% of Long-Term Visit Pass (LTVP) and Long-Term Visit Pass Plus (LTVP+) applications were rejected. The Ministry for Home Affairs declined to disclose specific rejection statistics for criminal convictions, conduct issues, or sponsorship income requirements, citing the need to prevent attempts to game the system. Consequently, detailed breakdowns of rejections based on these specific criteria and the existence of minimum income requirements were not provided in the public record.

results.title: Data on Resident and Non-resident Workers Performing Shift Work
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower stated that the overall proportion of full-time employees on shift work was 9.4% in 2022 and 6.5% in 2024, with 2023 data unavailable due to biennial collection. Data regarding residency status and specific rest hours are not available. The top five sectors for shift work in 2024 are accommodation, petroleum/chemical/pharmaceutical products, electronic/computer/optical products, air transport, and security services. No further details on daily or inter-shift rest hours were provided for these sectors.

results.title: Data on Seniors Aged 65 and Above Living Alone and Plans for New Initiatives to Prevent Unattended Deaths
results.summary: As of 2024, approximately 87,000 residents aged 65 and above lived alone in Singapore. To prevent unattended deaths, the Government relies on active befriending programmes, Silver Generation Office collaboration with grassroots volunteers, and outreach by Active Ageing Centres. The Minister stated these existing initiatives will be continued to address ageing-related issues. No new specific Government-led initiatives beyond these established efforts were announced in the response.

results.title: Data on Singaporeans Using MediSave for Family Members by Income Profile and Potential Risk of Insufficient MediSave Funds
results.summary: Between 2022 and 2024, approximately 210,000 Singaporeans annually utilized MediSave for family members across diverse income profiles, though individual healthcare needs remain the primary usage. The Minister noted that risks of insufficient funds typically arise from personal medical expenses rather than family support. Government support mechanisms, including top-ups, Additional Premium Support, and MediFund, are available to assist those with lower balances or exhausted funds. No individual loses coverage due to inability to pay.

results.title: Data on Wait Times, Referral Sources, Staffing and Re-engagement Rates for Youth Mental Health Services over Last Five Years
results.summary: For public child and adolescent mental health services at NUH and IMH, median wait times from referral to first appointment were 34 days in 2020 and 26 days in 2024, while 90th to 95th percentile times decreased from 94 to 84 days respectively. In 2024, the majority of referrals originated from public healthcare polyclinics, supplemented by general practitioners and community services. Staffing remains stable with each psychiatrist managing 1,300 to 1,500 outpatient attendances annually. No-show rates declined from approximately 22% in 2020 to 14% in 2024, supported by re-engagement actions such as pre-appointment confirmation calls and telephone follow-ups for missed appointments.

results.title: Deceased Flat Owners with Arrears Owed to Town Councils
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that while the Ministry monitors Town Councils' overall arrears rates, it does not possess the granular data required to answer specific questions regarding deceased flat owners. Consequently, the Minister could not provide the number of deceased owners with arrears, the proportion of cases exceeding nine months, or the total value of such debts. The requested statistics on deceased owners specifically remain unavailable to the Ministry.

results.title: Demographics of Food Delivery Platform Workers and Foreign Worker Impact on Incomes of Local Platform Workers
results.summary: In 2024, 15,300 Singapore residents (13,400 Citizens and 1,900 PRs) worked as food and parcel delivery platform workers. The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) does not track foreign food delivery workers because Work Passes are not issued for this sector; only Singaporeans and PRs are eligible as platform workers. MOM enforces against illegal foreign work and abetting locals, while legitimate outsourcing to third-party logistics companies hiring foreigners remains capped at the industry's dependency ratio ceiling. These outsourced workers hold fixed monthly salaries under employer-employee relationships rather than per-job remuneration. Platform operators have agreed to provide clarity on outsourcing practices, encourage workers to target high-demand areas, and require outsourced firms to submit Work Pass documentation for audits.

results.title: Deployment of EV Chargers in HDB Car Parks and Requests for Additional Chargers
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority reports over 25,000 EV charging points island-wide, with more than 7,500 installed across approximately 85% of HDB car parks. The agency is on track to meet its target of 60,000 points by 2030 and aims to provide convenient access to an EV charger for every HDB resident by year-end. Deployment will continue in high-demand car parks where electrical capacity permits, with the LTA monitoring utilisation rates to guide future scaling efforts.

results.title: Design and Installation of Smaller Lifts for Lift Upgrading Programme in Choa Chu Kang Ave 3
results.summary: Under the Lift Upgrading Programme (LUP), the Housing and Development Board installed smaller-capacity lifts in low-rise blocks like Choa Chu Kang Avenue 3 based on technical feasibility and cost-effectiveness. These lifts are wide enough for standard wheelchairs but cannot accommodate Singapore Civil Defence Force stretchers, prompting the use of alternative evacuation methods such as soft carrying canvases or staircases. For all LUP projects, including this one, lift dimensions were communicated to residents via brochures and posters during the polling process. Future projects will continue this holistic design approach while ensuring stakeholder consultation.

results.title: Dialogue Sessions and Workshops for Students, Youths and Businesses to Improve Foreign Policy Understanding
results.summary: Since January 2024, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs conducted approximately 150 dialogue sessions and workshops for students, youths, and businesses, with attendance ranging from 20 to 1,000 participants per event. Effectiveness is measured through participant feedback on message clarity and format utility, guiding continuous improvements to content and delivery. Beyond physical engagements, the Ministry provides digital content on social media to reach wider audiences and is exploring interactive small group workshops for deeper discussions. These initiatives aim to explain foreign policy fundamentals while gathering public views.

results.title: Distinguishing between Malay and Indian Muslim Food Providers in Bidding Criteria for HDB Rental Coffee Shops
results.summary: Since 2005, all new or vacant HDB rental coffee shops must include at least one halal stall to ensure resident access and inclusivity, without distinguishing between Malay or Indian Muslim cuisines. Operators retain full discretion to select the specific halal cuisine served to meet local demand, resulting in a diverse mix of options across 19 Sengkang rental coffee shops. While eight stalls serve Indian-Muslim cuisine and five serve Malay/Muslim options, the remaining three halal stalls ceased operations this year. HDB is actively assisting operators in securing replacements within the tenancy period, while residents also access halal food through nearby hawker centres, food courts, and shopping malls.

results.title: Educational Tours on Offshore Fish Farms and Kelongs to Increase Appreciation for Local Produce
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment stated that visits to offshore fish farms and kelongs are not encouraged due to public safety and biosecurity constraints. While the Singapore Food Agency collaborates with the Ministry of Education and other agencies to raise awareness of local produce benefits, a formal framework for educational tours on these sites is not being established. The agency continues to support local farm growth through technology adoption rather than facilitating public access to offshore facilities.

results.title: Effects of US Peace Initiatives and Israeli Military Actions on Singapore's Diplomatic Efforts
results.summary: The Minister commended US-led efforts alongside Qatar, Egypt, and Türkiye for securing a Gaza ceasefire and hostage release. Singapore has contributed S$24 million in humanitarian aid to Gaza, facilitated by strong regional relations with Israel, Jordan, Egypt, the UAE, and Qatar. The Ministry will continue providing updates on the evolving Middle East situation. The response did not specify Singapore's evaluation of US peace initiatives or Israeli military actions on national interests, nor did it detail the specific navigation between humanitarian obligations and diplomatic relations with Israel.

results.title: Efficacy of Trap-Neuter-Rehome/Release-Manage Programme and Sterilisation Rates of Community Cats in Managing and Controlling Community Cat Population
results.summary: The National Parks Board manages approximately 13,000 free-roaming cats in publicly accessible areas using the Trap-Neuter-Rehome/Release-Manage Programme. The agency targets a sterilisation rate of at least 70% by the end of 2030 to ensure sustainable population control. The Minister did not specify the exact population figure underpinning planning, current sterilisation rates versus annual kitten births, or the specific timeline for when sterilisation will outpace reproduction.

results.title: CareShield Life and Long-Term Care (Amendment) Bill
results.summary: The Government accepts all CareShield Life Council recommendations and introduces the CareShield Life and Long-Term Care (Amendment) Bill to strengthen premium recovery and reinstates underwriting criteria for Optional Cohorts and new citizens born in 1979 or earlier. While acknowledging concerns about payout adequacy, premium affordability for middle-income families, and gender-differentiated pricing, the Ministry clarifies that the Actuarial construct remains an objective measure where loss of three Activities of Daily Living indicates severe disability. To ensure no one loses coverage due to inability to pay, the Government will fully cover remaining premiums for those in dire financial situations after subsidies, though tele-assessments remain a pilot due to safety and technical constraints. The Bill also allows digital demand notes and confirms that multiple financial support layers exist for those excluded from the scheme.

results.title: Engaging US Government on FTA Immigration Commitments and Advocating for Rights of Singaporeans with US Visas
results.summary: The H-1B1 visa programme for Singaporean skilled labour remains unchanged, with the US Presidential Proclamation of 19 September 2025 applying separately and not affecting this specific category. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs will continue monitoring the situation and engaging US counterparts, noting no confirmed impact on bona fide Singaporean visa holders to date. However, possession of a valid US visa does not guarantee automatic entry or the full validity period of stay, as each country retains authority over its immigration policies. Singaporeans are advised to remain aware that existing visa statuses are subject to individual country determinations.

results.title: Engineering and Operational Criteria to Differentiate Isolated Incidents from Underlying Systemic Issues in Rail Network
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority (LTA) diagnoses rail incidents by analyzing fault trends, reliability indicators, system logs, asset conditions, and maintenance records. Recent disruptions across different Mass Rapid Transit lines in July to September have different causes, with investigations indicating similar issues are unlikely to recur on other lines. To further verify the absence of systemic problems, the Rail Reliability Taskforce has been formed to review recent incidents and jointly audit the condition, maintenance practices, and operations of critical systems.

results.title: Ensuring Manned Service Counters at Banks for Less Tech-savvy Seniors' Banking Needs
results.summary: The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) does not collect data on the ratio of manned bank counters to senior residents and will not mandate a minimum ratio. Instead, MAS emphasizes to banks the importance of providing adequate support, including adequate branch coverage, dedicated senior queues, and digital literacy programmes with community partners. Banks are expected to consider local elderly demographics when planning operations and deploy Digital Ambassadors proficient in mother tongue languages to assist seniors with banking needs at branches.

results.title: Establishment of Mosques within Pasir Ris-Changi Group Representation Constituency
results.summary: The Acting Minister-in-charge of Muslim Affairs stated there are currently no plans to establish new mosques within Pasir Ris-Changi GRC. The existing community is served by several facilities, including Al-Istighfar in Pasir Ris, Darul Ghufran in Tampines, and Al-Taqua in Bedok. Additionally, the upcoming mosque in Tampines North will provide future capacity. To manage peak demand, these mosques have implemented multiple prayer sessions for Fridays and Hari Raya. The Islamic Religious Council of Singapore (MUIS) continues to review mosque capacity to ensure adequate provision of prayer spaces for the Muslim community.

results.title: Expansion Plans for Sengkang Public Library
results.summary: Under the Libraries of the Future Masterplan, the National Library Board ensures branch libraries are supported by larger regional hubs rather than standalone expansions. Sengkang Library received an 18% size increase during its 2017 revamp, while Punggol Library was subsequently opened at the One Punggol integrated hub. The NLB will continuously review collections and services to meet community needs, with no specific plans for further interim expansion at Sengkang currently stated.

results.title: Expediting Completion of HDB Developments with Longer Waiting Time
results.summary: To accelerate home ownership, HDB prioritizes immediate construction sites, adopts innovative procurement, and expedites approvals, resulting in close to 70% of flats launched in 2023–2025 having waiting times of four years or less. The Board committed to launching more Shorter Waiting Time flats (under three years), increasing supply from 2,800 last year to 4,690 this year, exceeding the 4,500 commitment. While contractors are incentivized to finish early for capital recovery, sites with complex preparatory works may still require longer timelines. To support buyers facing delays, HDB ramped up the Parenthood Provisional Housing Scheme (PPHS) supply from 800 in 2021 to approximately 4,000 by year-end, offering temporary housing for eligible families while they await their BTO flats.

results.title: Extending MediSave Coverage to Funeral-related Expenses and Posthumous Expenditures
results.summary: Medisave cannot be used for wake-related expenses as it is strictly intended for healthcare needs, not posthumous expenditures. While MediSave contributions are sized for old-age healthcare, the government maintains this distinction. However, individuals with reduced life expectancy or terminal illness may withdraw CPF/Medisave savings to meet various needs, including funeral costs. Additionally, remaining MediSave funds of deceased members are distributed to nominees in cash, and welfare organisations assist needy families with funeral expenses.

results.title: Facilitating Cross-border Taxi Transfers between Singapore and Johor While Safeguarding Singapore P2P Drives' Livelihoods
results.summary: The Ministry of Transport and Land Transport Authority are reviewing the Cross Border Taxi Scheme to liberalise drop-off points in Singapore and Johor Bahru while enforcing strict measures against illegal point-to-point services. Discussions with Malaysia include increasing taxi quotas, introducing larger vehicle models, and adopting ride-hailing apps for bookings, alongside plans to install on-board units for location tracking. To safeguard livelihoods, the Ministry will consult associations on reasonable fares that account for operational cost disparities and will strictly enforce against undercutting by illegal providers. Enforcement operations against illegal services will continue, including actions against unauthorised apps and chat groups promoting illicit cross-border trips.

results.title: Fiscal Prudence of Offsetting Grants for Enterprises and Impact of G7 Proposal to Exempt US-parented Groups from Global Minimum Tax Rules
results.summary: All economic incentives, including the Refundable Investment Credit, require recipients to meet specific conditions such as job creation and local investment; failure to comply allows the Government to terminate support and claw back grants. Singapore implemented the Domestic Top-up Tax and Multinational Enterprise Top-up Tax from 1 January 2025 to prevent revenue cession under Pillar Two rules. Regarding the G7's June 2025 proposal for a "side-by-side" solution exempting US-parented groups, the impact on Singapore's fiscal outlook remains uncertain as discussions on the framework's details are ongoing. The Government is monitoring the issue closely and will review its approach once greater clarity emerges regarding other jurisdictions' responses and the eventual outcome of these negotiations.

results.title: Food Waste Segregation Programme for Households Nation-wide
results.summary: Food waste remains a priority under the Zero Waste Masterplan, with current efforts focused on industrial and commercial generators rather than a nationwide household programme. While community partners like Public Waste Collectors have trialled household collection, sustained participation proved difficult without broader stakeholder support. The Ministry encourages ground-up initiatives and will continue reviewing measures to reduce domestic food waste, but no specific timeline for a mandatory household segregation scheme was announced.

results.title: Formal Tracking of Intergenerational Wealth Transfers in Resale Property Purchases towards Housing Affordability
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the Ministry does not formally track the proportion of private and HDB resale property purchases funded by intergenerational wealth transfers. Consequently, no official assessment of the impact of such transfers on housing affordability in either the private residential or HDB resale markets is available. The requested data and analysis remain untracked by the agency.

results.title: Funding and Viewership Ratings for Public Service Broadcasting Programmes
results.summary: The Government allocates approximately $380 million annually to Mediacorp for overall operations rather than individual programmes, a figure lower than Finland and Denmark's national broadcasters but sufficient to reach over 90% of the local population. While TV reach has declined by about 10% over the last decade, unique video viewership on meWATCH has increased by 80%, with public satisfaction remaining steady at more than 75%. To expand the reach of local content, the Infocomm Media Development Authority launched the International Co-Production Fund two years ago to support local producers in collaborating with international counterparts like Netflix and Tencent. This initiative aims to create content with audiences beyond Singapore while anchoring themes in local stories, with ongoing assessment of the fund's sustainability and potential expansion.

results.title: Government Investment in Healthcare Technology R&D in Last Three Years and Support for Research Cooperation between Clinicians and Engineers
results.summary: The Government does not track specific R&D expenditure figures for technology-enabled care for the elderly and disabled over the last three years. However, significant funding is available under the Research, Innovation and Enterprise Plan, specifically within the Human Health and Potential Domain, to support population health and longevity research. Interdisciplinary collaborations between clinicians and engineers are facilitated through the Open Fund – Large Collaborative Grant and various open innovation platforms. These initiatives encourage partnerships across academia and industry to accelerate the development and implementation of clinical innovations.

results.title: Health Risks Arising from Non-prescribed Performance-enhancing Drug Use
results.summary: Androgenic steroids and human growth hormones are prescription-only medicines that cause serious side effects like stroke and organ damage when used without medical supervision. The Health Sciences Authority's Enforcement Branch handles illegal supply reports, while Anti-Doping Singapore collaborates with Institutes of Higher Learning to deliver anti-doping education outreach programmes to student-athletes. These initiatives aim to increase awareness regarding the consequences of abusing performance-enhancing drugs. Effectiveness and behavioural outcomes of these programmes will be assessed following their roll-out.

results.title: Health Risks of Tick-borne Diseases in Singapore and Mitigating Spread of Diseases
results.summary: Tick-borne diseases like Lyme disease and severe fever with thrombocytopenia syndrome are not common in Singapore, though imported cases among travellers pose a risk. Sustained community transmission remains low. The Communicable Diseases Agency (CDA) educates travellers on prevention abroad and advises seeking medical treatment upon returning ill. CDA collaborates with the National Parks Board and National Environment Agency using a One Health approach for surveillance and research. Non-specific symptoms include fever, muscle aches, and rash; untreated cases can cause severe complications.

results.title: Higher Citizenships and Permanent Residencies Granted Last Year
results.summary: The Minister explained that the increase in new Permanent Residents and Citizenships last year reflects a carefully controlled intake dependent on application quality, specific sector needs like healthcare, and a stronger applicant pool demonstrating assimilability and contributions. Granting decisions also consider demographic trends, with future adjustments reserved for significant shifts such as declining fertility rates. The government maintains a measured immigration pace, prioritizing the integration and commitment of new residents to ensure Singapore remains a cohesive society. The Minister did not confirm whether this year's increase indicates a sustained upward trend, noting instead that annual numbers vary based on changing national needs and application volumes.

results.title: Impact of Climate Change and Rising Temperatures on Structural Condition and Appearance of Older Buildings
results.summary: Rising heat and intense rainfall from climate change may increase spalling concrete, peeling paint, and mould growth in aging buildings, including HDB flats. The Building and Construction Authority mandates façade inspections every seven years for older buildings over 20 years old and 13 metres tall, with structural inspections required every ten years for all residential buildings. Town Councils conduct Repair and Redecoration works to address defects like peeling surfaces and mould, potentially accelerating these works based on building conditions. Regular inspections, timely repairs, and technologies like drones ensure structural safety despite expected wear and tear.

results.title: Electronic Conveyancing and Other Matters Bill
results.summary: The Government proposes amending legislation to digitalise property conveyancing and enable remote witnessing for Lasting Power of Attorney (LPA) execution, aiming to reduce reliance on wet-ink signatures and physical meetings. The Electronic Conveyancing and Other Matters Bill introduces a new paragraph 1A in the Mental Capacity Act, allowing remote witnessing for donors aged 75 and below while retaining in-person options. Safeguards include written declarations by Certificate Issuers regarding donor understanding and absence of undue pressure, without mandatory video recordings. The Digital Conveyancing Portal will supplement existing methods, linking to the Land Register to prevent multiple property options. Concerns regarding scam risks, data privacy, and system downtime will be addressed through cybersecurity standards and unified guidance notes for issuers.

results.title: Impact of Illegal Hawker Stall Subletting on Food Affordability and Ensuring Fair Access for New Entrants
results.summary: The National Environment Agency (NEA) opposes illegal hawker stall subletting to prevent rent-seeking that could raise operating costs and reduce food affordability. NEA conducts regular inspections, issuing warnings to non-operating stallholders and termination notices or rental bans for recalcitrant cases. The public is encouraged to report suspected subletting activities. To ensure fair access, NEA maintains an open, transparent bidding system for its managed hawker centres where prospective tenderers must personally operate stalls. These requirements are explicitly stated in tenancy agreements, and bids are monitored for anomalies to preserve entry opportunities for new hawkers.

results.title: Impact of US Government Shutdown on Singapore and Mitigation Strategies
results.summary: The Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Trade and Industry stated that the US government shutdown has not yet caused macroeconomic impact on Singapore, though prolonged shutdowns could dampen US demand for exports. To mitigate potential risks, the Government offers the Enterprise Development Grant for business transformation and the Business Adaptation Grant via the Singapore Economic Resilience Taskforce (SERT) for supply chain optimization under US tariff measures. For individuals, the SkillsFuture Jobseeker Support scheme assists involuntarily unemployed jobseekers, while upskilling programmes and the GRaduate Industry Traineeships programme enhance employability for workers and fresh graduates. The Government will continue monitoring developments and adapting responses as necessary.

results.title: Impact on Singapore from Shutdown Affecting Operations and Inspective Services at US Food and Drug Administration
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment stated that Singapore's food and medicine safety remains secure despite potential US FDA disruptions. The Singapore Food Agency (SFA) mandates accreditation for high-risk food importers and enforces inspections at critical supply chain nodes, rejecting unsafe products immediately. Concurrently, the Health Sciences Authority (HSA) verifies all medicines against rigorous international standards and Good Manufacturing Practice before market entry. Post-marketing, HSA conducts risk-based sampling and operates a national adverse reaction monitoring programme to detect safety issues. Both agencies retain the authority to take swift regulatory actions to protect public health, ensuring imported goods meet all safety requirements regardless of foreign operational changes.

results.title: Impact on Singapore's Social Fabric when Religious Leaders are Trained Overseas
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth stated that religious leaders must maintain social harmony regardless of their training location, while welcoming efforts to train teachers grounded in the Singaporean context. Specific local initiatives include programmes at Trinity Theological College, St Francis Xavier Major Seminary, and the Buddhist College of Singapore. Additionally, the Islamic Religious Council of Singapore (MUIS) offers the Postgraduate Certificate in Islam in Contemporary Societies (PCICS) and is establishing the Singapore College of Islamic Studies (SCIS) to train future asatizah locally. To strengthen social cohesion, the Ministry promotes continual social mixing through Racial and Religious Harmony Circles, which organise events and dialogues to build trust among different racial and religious groups.

results.title: Imposing Accountability on Social Media Platforms to Prevent Impersonation Scams
results.summary: The Singapore Police Force issued an Implementation Directive to Meta, requiring enhanced measures to prevent impersonation scams involving government officials. These mandates include stopping the publication of officials' images in scam ads, removing impersonating accounts, and prioritizing Singaporean user reports. The Ministry of Home Affairs and SPF will collaborate with Meta to extend global protection measures to other influential public figures. The Government will consider imposing similar requirements on other online platforms based on the evolving scam situation.

results.title: Integrating Depression and Suicide Prevention Screenings into Primary Care Visits for Seniors
results.summary: The Minister for Health stated that international evidence is insufficient to recommend routine depression and suicide screenings for asymptomatic adults. Consequently, primary care visits for seniors will not integrate these screenings as a standard opt-in measure. Instead, doctors are trained to address depression or suicide ideation only when signs are present during follow-ups on presenting medical complaints. No initiatives were announced to make such screenings opt-out, as routine screening is not currently recommended.

results.title: Long-term Cost-benefit Analysis of Singapore's Electrification Efforts on Bus Fares
results.summary: The Ministry plans to replace only buses nearing end-of-life with electric models, noting that life cycle costs are comparable to diesel buses as higher procurement expenses are offset by lower fuel and maintenance costs. The Acting Minister stated that accelerating the transition to electric buses may become feasible without significantly impacting public transport fares as technology improves and costs decline. This approach supports Singapore's net zero emissions commitment while improving air quality and public health. No specific long-term cost-benefit analysis figures or detailed safeguards against fare increases were provided in the response.

results.title: Lowering Speed Limit for Bicycles on Shared Paths to Enhance Safety
results.summary: The Ministry maintains the 25 km/h speed limit for bicycles on shared paths, citing it as the maximum allowed speed derived from Active Mobility Advisory Panel consultations rather than a mandatory riding pace. Cyclists must slow down and give way to pedestrians, particularly in crowded areas or when obstacles are present, while adhering to the rule that pedestrians keep left to allow overtaking. For narrower footpaths, the limit is reduced to 10 km/h, restricting usage to manual bicycles and personal mobility aids for those with mobility issues. The Land Transport Authority continues education campaigns since 2016 targeting community groups and schools to promote safe behaviour, with enforcement actions reserved for reckless riders. The Ministry does not plan to build cycling paths on roads due to space constraints but will continue developing off-road infrastructure where feasible.

results.title: Measures to Detect and Deter Incidents That May Provoke Racial or Religous Disharmony
results.summary: The Ministry of Home Affairs maintains a robust legal framework, including the Penal Code, Maintenance of Religious Harmony Act, and Maintenance of Racial Harmony Act, to deter incidents threatening racial or religious harmony. Specific threats linked to places of worship trigger immediate police action, such as investigations and increased patrols. In the last two years, police investigated approximately 25 race- and religion-related offences annually. Upstream efforts under the SGSecure movement enhance religious organisations' crisis response capabilities through partnerships with the Police and Ministry of Culture, Community and Youth. Community-level initiatives involve the Safety and Security Watch Group conducting security surveys and delivering training. The Racial and Religious Harmony Circles foster understanding via community celebrations and dialogues. Following the Al-Istiqamah Mosque incident, faith leaders condemned the act and expressed solidarity, reinforcing societal resilience against threats to harmony.

results.title: Measures to Enhance Housing Mobility for Seniors
results.summary: The Housing and Development Board will launch five Community Care Apartments since 2021, including a new project in Sengkang for the October 2025 Build-To-Order exercise and another in Toa Payoh next year. Seniors remain eligible for short-lease 2-room flexi flats if they have not previously purchased one, with at least 40% of such flats set aside in each BTO project. From 1 December 2025, the Silver Housing Bonus increases to a maximum of $40,000 for those right-sizing to 2-room flats or CCAs, extends to seniors in private properties with annual values between $21,000 and $31,000, and applies if seniors commit to a net increase in their CPF Retirement Account after right-sizing.

results.title: Measures to Manage High-risk Individuals Exhibiting Violence towards Animals
results.summary: The Minister clarified that Courts may impose Mandatory Treatment Orders requiring offenders to undergo psychiatric treatment, such as at the Institute of Mental Health, for animal cruelty and abuse cases. This measure addresses violent behaviour towards animals by mandating rehabilitation programmes alongside existing penalties. Coordination with the Animal and Veterinary Service and community partners supports managing high-risk individuals, though the transcript did not specify if mandatory counselling can be imposed in addition to jail terms or fines.

results.title: Median Out-of-pocket Bill Amounts for Common Chronic Conditions and Day Surgeries at Public Health Institutions
results.summary: Effective 1 April 2025, the refresh of MediShield Life and MediSave limits eliminates out-of-pocket payments for median subsidised day surgery bills at public healthcare institutions. For Chronic Disease Management Programme (CDMP) conditions, the median annual out-of-pocket cost remains around $50 per patient after subsidies and MediSave. The Ministry of Health publishes annual hospital bill data for surgical procedures and inpatient conditions, broken down by ward settings to aid patient choice. MediShield Life, MediSave, and Community Health Assist Scheme (CHAS) subsidies are reviewed periodically to ensure adequacy amid rising costs. The Ministry will study extending bill transparency to additional conditions.

results.title: Monitoring Performance Standards of School Bus Operators
results.summary: The Ministry of Education sets performance standards for school bus operators covering safety, reliability, and service quality, while social service agencies establish tailored requirements for special education schools. All operators must comply with Land Transport Authority regulations. Schools award contracts via competitive bidding, prioritizing fare competitiveness alongside non-price factors such as track records, fleet capacity, and residential coverage. Individual schools verify compliance with these standards in partnership with operators.

results.title: Monitoring Safety and Maintenance of Road Conditions in Areas with Heavy Construction Activity
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority (LTA) monitors road safety in construction-heavy areas like Woodlands through regular inspections using video analytics to detect defects and determine resurfacing needs. Critical defects, such as potholes, are repaired within a day. Beyond current methods, LTA is considering deploying cameras on public buses to identify road defects and is collaborating with industry partners and higher education institutes to research more durable, sustainable road mixes. These initiatives aim to minimize road defects and reduce the frequency of maintenance requirements.

results.title: Number of CPF Members Aged 65-67 Still in Employment and Review of Dependants' Protection Scheme to Increase Age Limit of 65
results.summary: As of 2024, 94,500 CPF members aged 65 to 67 are in employment, representing 33% of that cohort. The Government raised the Dependants' Protection Scheme (DPS) age limit from 60 to 65 in 2021 but currently does not plan to extend it further due to disproportionately higher premiums driven by mortality rates after age 65, which would reduce retirement savings. While the Minister confirmed that actuarial studies are ongoing to align coverage with the potential retirement age increase to 65 before 2030, extending coverage beyond 65 remains constrained by sustainability and affordability. DPS lapse rates have remained low at less than 0.2% over the past three years, with approximately 40% of lapsed members reinstating coverage by the end of 2024.

results.title: Operating Surplus and Business Costs Metrics for Overall Economy and across Manufacturing and Services Industries from 2016 to 2024
results.summary: The Department of Statistics does not compile data on unit net or gross operating surplus. Available data covers only unit business costs (UBC) for the manufacturing and services sectors from 2016 to the first half of 2025, indexed to 2016. UBC figures for the overall economy, outward-oriented services, and domestic-oriented services industries are not available. Consequently, the requested metrics for operating surplus and specific service industry breakdowns cannot be provided.

results.title: Outcome of Police Investigations on SPH Media's Overstatement of Circulation Figures in 2023
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs stated that the investigation into SPH Media Holdings' 2023 overstatement of circulation figures remains ongoing. No specific outcome, timeline, or penalty has been announced at this stage. The police report referenced by the Board of SPH Media Holdings is currently under review, and further updates will be provided once the inquiry concludes.

results.title: Oversight of Issues Related To Payment and Incentive Schemes between Platform Operators and Platform Worker Associations
results.summary: The Platform Workers Trilateral Group agreed that payment and incentive issues should be addressed bilaterally between platform operators and worker associations due to unique business models. To guide these discussions, NTUC, associations, and operators announced three industry-wide principles on 11 September 2025 covering fair earnings, visibility on structural changes, and reasonable incentive structures. This approach builds on the formalisation of platform work associations under the Platform Workers Act. The principles were not made public by the Ministry but were announced by NTUC.

results.title: Owners Opting to Sell Flat's Lease under HDB Lease Buyback Scheme and Whether Their Flats Will be Offered VERS
results.summary: From January to June 2025, 1,170 senior households joined the Lease Buyback Scheme (LBS) to sell a portion of their flat's lease while retaining residence. The Minister clarified that seniors participating in LBS need not worry about future Voluntary Early Redevelopment Scheme (VERS) eligibility. Should a block joining LBS later undergo VERS, the Government will ensure participants receive a fair package accounting for the value of their retained lease. This measure allows seniors to unlock home value for CPF top-ups and monthly payouts without compromising future redevelopment benefits.

results.title: Planned Studies to Assess Developmental Impact of Grow Well SG
results.summary: The Ministry plans to assess Grow Well SG's impact by tracking children's screen time, physical activity, and sleep, alongside surveys measuring parental awareness of healthy habits. These longitudinal indicators will be monitored continuously to refine initiatives and resources. Findings will be shared publicly when ready to inform future policy development. The response confirms ongoing monitoring but does not specify exact timelines for study completion or the precise date when insights will be released.

results.title: Plans for Attracting Younger Stallholders to Ensure Viability of Wet Market Operations
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment stated that wet market viability depends primarily on adequate consumer demand rather than solely on attracting younger stallholders. She noted that declining patronage and shifting demographics have reduced the number of Singaporeans shopping at wet markets in favor of supermarkets and online platforms. Consequently, the government views the availability of younger stallholders as secondary to ensuring sufficient market demand for continued operation.

results.title: Plans for Parenthood Provisional Housing Scheme Voucher Beyond December 2025
results.summary: The Minister for National Development confirmed there are no plans to extend the Parenthood Provisional Housing Scheme (PPHS) Voucher beyond December 2025. This temporary measure supports eligible families renting HDB flats or open market bedrooms while the Ministry ramps up PPHS supply to 4,000 units by year-end. The decision to discontinue the voucher follows the successful rollout where all married applicants with at least one child aged 18 or below, including expectant parents, were offered PPHS flat selection opportunities. Consequently, the voucher will cease as the increased supply meets demand.

results.title: Plans for Temporary Facilities Built for World Aquatics Championship 2025
results.summary: Following the World Aquatics Championships 2025, the WCH Arena's usage will be extended until 2030 to complement ActiveSG facilities. The venue will serve public swimming, national team training, and host future events like the 2029 Southeast Asian Games. Public access is scheduled to commence by end October. The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth confirmed these plans after a feasibility study, noting positive feedback from stakeholders. Further details regarding operational specifics will be shared in the coming weeks.

results.title: Plans to Review Continuation of Specialised Schools for Full Subject-based Banding
results.summary: The Ministry confirmed that Crest and Spectra Secondary Schools remain relevant under Full Subject-Based Banding due to their unique, practice-oriented curriculum for students with specific learning styles. These schools offer both Secondary Education Certificate (SEC) subjects and Institute of Technical Education Skills Subject Certificates, with the ability to deliver certain SEC subjects at G2-level based on student aptitude. To support this specialised profile, the schools receive enhanced resourcing, including additional funding and improved teacher-to-student ratios, distinct from standard secondary schools.

results.title: Potential Solution for Traffic Congestion in McNair and Towner Precinct due to Multiple Upcoming BTO Developments
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority will implement a right-turn from McNair Road onto Balestier Road towards the Central Expressway, alongside junction widening and lane extensions, to mitigate traffic from new Build-To-Order developments in the McNair and Towner precinct. These enhancements are scheduled for rollout before the first half of 2026, when the new housing completions are expected to increase car traffic. The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed these measures will address congestion prior to the anticipated rise in volume.

results.title: Practice of Motor Insurers Reducing Third-party Damages Awards to Accident Victims by Deducting Sums Paid Out from Victims' MediShield Life
results.summary: The Singapore Courts have established a legal precedent prohibiting motor insurers from deducting MediShield Life payouts from third-party damages awarded to accident victims. This ruling relies on the Insurance Exception to the common law rule against double recovery, aligning with doctrines in other common law jurisdictions like the United States and United Kingdom. Consequently, no new legislation is required as this legal principle already addresses the concern. The Ministry of Health does not possess data on the prevalence of this practice among insurers.

results.title: Proposal for Additional Primary Schools in Bishan East-Sin Ming Region
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated there are currently no plans to build new primary schools in the Bishan East-Sin Ming Division. This decision follows an assessment that sufficient school places exist within one to two kilometres of the area. The projected demand increase from new housing developments is largely offset by decreasing demand from mature parts of Bishan. MOE aims to avoid oversupply, which could lead to school vacancies and reduced programme diversity. The Ministry will continue monitoring demand as the resident profile changes.

results.title: Proposed Simplified Fast-track Claims Process under Assistive Technology Fund for Low-cost Digital Aids
results.summary: The Minister clarified that while no ATF applications exist for tracking devices like AirTags, a simplified process is already available for basic devices without requiring a detailed healthcare assessor evaluation. The Assistive Technology Fund provides means-tested subsidies for assistive devices supporting independent living, with applications assessed based on whether the device aids early intervention, education, employment, therapy, rehabilitation, or independent living, irrespective of device cost. Although the Minister noted the current multi-stage process may deter uptake for low-cost digital aids, no specific timeline for introducing a dedicated fast-track mechanism for such items was stated in the response.

results.title: Providing Stronger Incentives and Safeguards for Schools to Open Facilities for Community Use
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) partners with Sport Singapore and the People's Association to facilitate community usage of school facilities outside school hours under the MOE-SportSG Dual-Use Scheme (DUS). Currently, approximately 180 school fields and 220 indoor sports halls are actively utilized by the community, with 90% of Government schools participating in the scheme. To minimize administrative burdens, SportSG manages the booking process and assumes responsibility for facilities during community sharing hours to ensure cleanliness for students. The Ministry targets onboarding the remaining schools by early next year. While new schools are designed with sports facilities to facilitate community usage, and schools may allow private buses to park overnight, the response regarding specific incentives, safeguards, and a systematic nationwide framework was deferred to subsequent Sittings covering questions by Ms Elysa Chen and Ms Gho Sze Kee.

results.title: Public Education Efforts on Risks, Costs and Benefits of Using Stablecoins
results.summary: The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) will educate consumers via MoneySense on stablecoin operations, risks, and the distinction between regulated and unregulated products. MAS is preparing legislation to differentiate MAS-regulated stablecoins, backed by high-quality reserves, from unregulated crypto-assets. The Government assesses that growing SGD stablecoin usage is unlikely to affect currency stability or monetary policy, as issuance requires full backing by SGD cash or liquid assets, preventing new net SGD creation. While regulated stablecoins may enhance cross-border payment efficiency, consumers are advised that they are less safe than insured bank deposits and unsuitable for holding savings. Financial institutions are not permitted to promote unregulated crypto-assets to the general public.

results.title: Public Rental Flats to be Constructed Over Next Five Years
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that approximately 4,600 public rental flats are expected to be completed over the next five years. This figure represents the total number of new units the Ministry plans to construct within this period. No further details regarding specific locations, timelines for individual batches, or eligibility criteria were provided in the response.

results.title: Publishing Commuter Graciousness Index and Commuter Graciousness Survey Results
results.summary: The Ministry has not published the Commuter Graciousness Index or survey results for the last five years, citing an ongoing review of the Land Transport Authority's survey methodology. Instead, the Government encourages gracious behaviour through the Caring SG Commuter Movement and related campaigns. While the Ministry tracks perceptions of graciousness via LTA and Public Transport Council studies, no specific timeline for publishing historical data or implementing additional measures was stated. The Acting Minister noted a previous written reply on 26 September 2025 addressed similar inquiries.
