results.title: Rationale for Minimum Eligibility Criterion of At Least One Local Employee for Energy Efficiency Grant
results.summary: The Energy Efficiency Grant (EEG), introduced in September 2022, has approved nearly 15,800 applications by August 2025, projecting an estimated 10,700 tonnes of lifetime carbon abatement. While the EEG Base Tier does not require submitted energy savings data, the Advanced Tier mandates a demonstration of over 350 tonnes of lifetime carbon abatement endorsed by a whitelisted third-party assessor. Crucially, since June 2025, the minimum eligibility criterion requiring at least one local employee at the firm-level has been removed to include sole proprietors, particularly in the food and beverage sector. To maintain integrity without this criterion, administering agencies have implemented post-disbursement audits to ensure grants are correctly channelled to eligible companies with legitimate operations in Singapore.

results.title: Recruitment of Money Mules by Criminal Syndicates
results.summary: Starting this month, the Government implements a new facility restriction framework limiting scam mules' access to financial, telecommunications, and Singpass services if they have been warned, issued composition sums, prosecuted, convicted, or assessed as high-risk during investigation. These restrictions lift upon acquittal or when no longer required, though basic banking access for salaries and benefits remains available. Recruitment typically occurs via online messaging and social media, with the Police investigating over 3,500 suspects in the first half of 2025, charging more than 500. The Police will not charge individuals genuinely tricked into providing facilities, as the Attorney-General's Chambers assesses evidential thresholds and public interest for prosecution. Data on tricked individuals is not maintained, and affected persons may appeal restrictions to the Police.

results.title: Regulating Businesses Providing Will and Probate Courses
results.summary: The Ministry clarified that will and probate courses are not restricted to the legal profession. Businesses providing such courses cannot give legal advice or offer services of an advocate and solicitor unless conducted by one. Unauthorised persons holding themselves out as legal practitioners face criminal liability under the Legal Profession Act. The public is advised to research course providers' backgrounds before signing up and to engage an advocate and solicitor for legal advice. Information on legacy planning is available on the Government's My Legacy @ LifeSG website.

results.title: Regulation of Clinical Aesthetic Treatments
results.summary: The Ministry of Health confirmed that unauthorised provision of clinical aesthetic procedures is already penalised under the Healthcare Services Act (HCSA). Existing Ethical Guidelines require practitioners to screen patients for psychological or psychiatric conditions related to body image before treatment. The Ministry will continue monitoring the sector and reviewing penalty adequacy to ensure effective deterrence and public safety. No new stricter penalties, regulatory reviews, or mandatory psychological screening guidelines were announced at this time.

results.title: Review and Recalibration of Foreign Manpower Mix to Enable Better Opportunities for Local Workforce
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) conducts annual reviews of foreign workforce policies to ensure complementarity and protect local job opportunities. Key measures include updating Employment Pass (EP) and S Pass qualifying salaries to match local wage growth, with new EP applicants aged 23 required to earn at least S$5,600, significantly above the median wage for fresh graduates. Additionally, the Complementarity Assessment Framework is refreshed annually, and the Shortage Occupation List is adjusted based on industry shortages and local pipeline development efforts. These actions aim to maintain a balanced foreign workforce while supporting local employment prospects.

results.title: Reviewing Penalties and Powers for Seizure of Unauthorised Possession of Equipment Designed to Mix Controlled Substances into Vapes
results.summary: Under section 10A of the Misuse of Drugs Act, possessing or manufacturing equipment designed for mixing controlled substances into vapes is an offence punishable by up to 20 years' imprisonment, a $200,000 fine, or both, regardless of whether the tools are free of drugs. Central Narcotics Bureau officers may seize such items under section 24 as evidence. The Minister stated there is currently no need to review these penalties or seizure powers.

results.title: Risks Posed by Generative AI Models to Mental Health and Safety of Children and Youths
results.summary: Government agencies are assessing generative AI risks to children and youths while implementing existing safeguards. Under the Codes of Practice for Online Safety, designated app stores must enforce age assurance measures by 31 March 2026 to restrict access to age-inappropriate apps for under-18 users. The Model AI Governance Framework for Generative AI applies to youth safety, complemented by education initiatives including Cyber Wellness and Mental Health Education within the Ministry of Education's Character and Citizenship Education curriculum. Additionally, the Infocomm Media Development Authority's Digital Skills for Life framework now covers generative AI usage, and the Positive Use Guide on Technology and Social Media offers practical tips for healthy technology relationships.

results.title: Safeguards to Prevent Nursing and Lactation Rooms Misuse
results.summary: The Building and Construction Authority (BCA) states that usage of nursing and lactation rooms is managed by building owners and facility managers under the Code on Accessibility in the Built Environment. Owners may implement measures such as access controls, regular monitoring, and clear signage to prevent misuse, while encouraging civic-minded usage. The Minister did not confirm plans for new penalties or specific BCA-led enforcement actions, noting that operational management remains with individual building owners.

results.title: Scaling Supply of Ambulatory Services by Partnering Private Service Operators to Meet Demands of Growing Ageing Population
results.summary: The Ministry of Health partners with private and non-profit providers to deliver subsidised ambulatory services like Community Rehabilitation and Day Care for eligible seniors. Efforts are underway to coordinate these services with Active Ageing Centres and Home Personal Care to ensure smooth transitions and operational efficiency. The Ministry will continue reviewing and adjusting the capacity for Community Rehabilitation and Day Care to meet the needs of the ageing population.

results.title: Singapore's Assessment of 20-Point Peace Plan for Gaza
results.summary: Singapore welcomes the 9 October 2025 agreement on the first phase of the US 20-point peace plan, which mandates a ceasefire and hostage release, viewing it as a critical step toward a negotiated two-state solution. The Government will accelerate humanitarian assistance through existing partners like the Egyptian Red Crescent and World Food Programme, contingent on opening overland routes and ensuring staff safety. Singapore will enhance capacity-building for the Palestinian Authority via technical assistance and scholarships but deems it premature to commit to peacekeeping troops, a Transitional Authority role, or joining the 12-nation financial coalition without a UN Security Council resolution. The Minister declined requests to join the Board of Peace or deploy medical teams immediately, citing the need for ground-level coordination and realistic assessment of needs.

results.title: Singapore's Diplomatic Expansion Strategies in Africa and Latin America
results.summary: The Minister for Foreign Affairs stated that Singapore maintains 53 Overseas Missions and continually evaluates this network to advance national interests in Africa and Latin America. Due to resource and manpower constraints, the Ministry supplements its presence with Non-Resident Ambassadors (NRAs) from the private sector and academia who travel regularly to engage foreign governments. Future establishment of new missions will be considered based on specific national needs and available resources, rather than a fixed timeline or list of target countries.

results.title: Singapore-ASEAN Collaboration on Regional Disaster Management Capacity Building
results.summary: Singapore actively participates in ASEAN mechanisms to enhance regional disaster preparedness and resilience, including the ASEAN Agreement on Disaster Management and Emergency Response (AADMER) and the ASEAN Committee on Disaster Management (ACDM). The nation hosts the annual ASEAN Strategic Policy Dialogue on Disaster Management and co-chairs the ICT Advisory Group on Disaster Management. Through the Singapore Cooperation Programme, Singapore conducts capacity-building programmes on disaster risk reduction and climate resilience in collaboration with international partners like the United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction. These efforts focus on technical assistance, resource mobilisation, and sharing best practices to support disaster-prone countries in the region.

results.title: Standard Procedures for Teachers when Questioning Students about Serious Disciplinary Incidents
results.summary: The Minister for Education confirmed that schools follow established procedures to ensure student well-being during disciplinary investigations, including conducting interviews in safe, private spaces with familiar staff and providing social-emotional support via counsellors for visibly distressed students. While parents are informed when a student shows distress, current protocols do not mandate parental presence during school-based questioning for serious incidents like alleged theft. The Minister stated there is no mandatory requirement for a parent or guardian to be present to prevent mental intimidation, though schools monitor students for signs of distress throughout the process.

results.title: Statistics for Number of and Projected Bed Spaces at Commercial Dormitories
results.summary: Currently, there are approximately 250,000 commercial dormitory beds, with a projected net increase of 27,000 beds over the next few years. This growth includes two Government-owned purpose-built dormitories in Jalan Tukang and Sengkang West, expected to complete in the first quarter of 2026 and mid-2028 respectively. To boost supply, the Government extended expiring dormitory leases, increased allowable gross floor area for industrial developments from 40% to 49%, and facilitated new Factory-Converted Dormitories. Additionally, employers, particularly in construction, are encouraged to establish Construction Temporary Quarters or Temporary Occupation Licence Quarters to meet worker accommodation needs, with public sector agencies leading this initiative.

results.title: Statutes (Miscellaneous Amendments) Bill
results.summary: The Statutes (Miscellaneous Amendments) Bill introduces 29 clauses amending 29 Acts to update service provisions for digital documents, align definitions following the repeal of the Private Hospitals and Medical Clinics Act 1980, and channel penalties into the Consolidated Fund. Specific changes include authorizing warrants for Development Fund advances, permitting employer deductions for MediShield Life and CareShield Life premiums, and clarifying Maintenance of Parents Act eligibility by removing age limits for children under 21. The Bill also appoints an executive director for the Singapore Medical Council, extends Disciplinary Tribunal time limits by up to nine months, and expands law enforcement agency definitions. Additionally, it empowers HDB to designate a Second Minister for appeals, updates the Medical Registration Act to reflect Duke-NUS name changes, and clarifies Town Council powers regarding vehicle disposal on common property.

results.title: Stepping Up Regulation and Supervision of Cord Blood Bank Services
results.summary: The Ministry of Health confirmed that the Healthcare Services (Cord Blood Banking Service) regulations 2021 align with international standards to ensure cord blood unit quality and safety. Under this framework, MOH conducts regular audits and inspections covering collection, handling, processing, storage, and distribution, with any lapses investigated promptly. Cordlife was found to have fallen short of these standards, triggering immediate investigation and corrective actions. While MOH continues to work with Cordlife to enhance their quality management system, contractual matters regarding storage, retrieval, or compensation remain private commercial arrangements outside MOH's purview.

results.title: Strategies to Encourage Singaporeans to Update COVID-19 Vaccinations amid Dominance of XFG Variant
results.summary: The XFG variant, a descendant of Omicron, shows no evidence of causing more severe illness or deaths compared to previous variants. Consequently, the Ministry of Health recommends COVID-19 vaccinations for individuals aged 60 and above, medically vulnerable persons aged six months and above, and residents of aged care facilities. Other eligible residents aged six months and above may also receive the vaccine free of charge under the National Vaccination Programme. To enhance convenience, vaccinations are available at over 600 general practitioner clinics, 10 polyclinics, and via mobile teams deployed to selected heartland locations and care facilities. Eligible seniors will receive SMS invitations.

results.title: Stricter Enforcement of Vehicle Loan Regulations to Prevent 100% Financing Packages
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport clarified that while MAS regulates motor vehicle loans with loan-to-value limits of 60% for vehicles above $20,000 OMV and 70% for those at or below, alternative financing arrangements like leasing or lease-to-own schemes by car dealers remain unregulated. Consequently, these unregulated packages circumvent existing MAS restrictions, offering buyers no protection against loan problems. Buyers are strongly advised to obtain financing through regulated arrangements to ensure consumer protection. The Ministry has not indicated plans to review or strictly enforce regulations on these specific unregulated dealer practices.

results.title: Success Rate of First-time Applicants Eligible for Family Care Scheme (Proximity) in Securing HDB BTO and BTO and SBF Units
results.summary: In the July 2025 HDB Build-To-Order (BTO) and Sale of Balance Flats (SBF) exercises, approximately 60% of first-timer families and 20% of first-timer singles eligible for the Family Care Scheme (Proximity) received queue numbers. Among successful first-timer family applicants, roughly 80% were on their first attempt while 10% were on their second. The Ministry allocates 30% of BTO supply to this scheme and offers additional ballots for unsuccessful first-timers applying for standard 4-room or smaller flats. While priority is given to inter-generational caregivers, outcomes remain subject to ballot luck, particularly in popular projects with limited supply. The Ministry encourages applicants to consider projects with larger supplies to improve their chances.

results.title: Support Measures for Middle-income Households and Retirees Exempt from Other Help Schemes for Impending Rise in Electricity and Gas Tariffs
results.summary: For October to December 2025, electricity and town gas tariffs will rise slightly by 0.3% due to higher imported fuel costs. While most HDB households, including middle-income families and retirees, receive GST Vouchers and U-Save to offset bills, those in private properties without U-Save eligibility may seek assistance from local Social Service Offices. Under the SG60 package, retirees aged 60 and above receive $800 in vouchers, while those aged 21 to 59 receive $600 if retired after 55. Additionally, all Singaporean households are eligible for $800 in CDC vouchers, benefiting approximately 1.3 million households and 1.1 million adults aged 60 and above. The government is monitoring imported natural gas pricing closely and encourages energy conservation to sustain support measures long-term.

results.title: Supporting Caregivers to Obtain Time-off from Work to Accompany Parents for Medical Appointments
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower stated that Flexible Work Arrangements (FWAs) support frontline workers in accompanying parents for medical appointments. To formalize this, the Government and tripartite partners implemented the Tripartite Guidelines on FWA Requests in December 2024. These guidelines establish a process for employees to request one-off or sustained arrangements based on business grounds. Employees may also utilize statutory annual leave or voluntary employer provisions, such as additional caregiver leave, to meet their specific needs.

results.title: Survey on Reasons for Singaporeans and Permanent Residents to Hire Migrant Domestic Workers
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) periodically commissions surveys on Migrant Domestic Workers (MDWs), with the latest conducted in 2021. Findings detailing employer reasons for hiring are available on MOM's website. Demand for MDWs caring for seniors has generally increased due to Singapore's ageing population. The Government will continue monitoring population trends and reviewing policies to address evolving household needs. Specific future demand projections were not provided in the response.

results.title: Take-up Rate for Physical CDC Vouchers and Reasons for Cessation of Physical Vouchers Issuance
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth confirmed that notification letters for the May 2025 CDC vouchers tranche were discontinued to support environmental sustainability, following a review of consistently high claim rates averaging about 97% since 2021. Despite the cessation of letters, public awareness was maintained through media announcements, social media, street banners, and digital displays, while assistance for digitally less-savvy residents remains available at community centres and SG Digital Community Hubs. The Minister stated that claim rates for the May 2025 tranche remain comparable to previous tranches, indicating resident familiarity with the digital claim process. Regarding the suggestion to roll over unused vouchers, the Minister reiterated that CDC vouchers are intended for daily expenses, with unused balances eligible for donation to public charities rather than carry-over to future tranches.

results.title: Take-up Rate of Plans under Advance Care Planning
results.summary: As of September 2025, over 87,000 Advance Care Plans (ACPs) have been completed, doubling the 2023 baseline under the "Plan Your Legacy Today!" campaign. The breakdown shows 94% of plans are by individuals aged 50+, 57% by females, and 82% by Chinese residents, with Malay and Indian residents comprising 7% and 4% respectively. Since June 2025, the myACP digital tool has facilitated 4,350 online completions for free. The Ministry continues targeted outreach via Active Ageing Centres and GP clinics to promote completion among healthy individuals.

results.title: Tiered Approach for MediSave Withdrawal Limits
results.summary: The Ministry of Health confirms that MediSave withdrawal limits for the Chronic Disease Management Programme (CDMP) are already tiered based on condition complexity: $500 for a single non-complex condition and $700 for complex cases involving two or more CDMP conditions. While the Minister noted that MediSave limits are regularly reviewed to balance current needs against future savings, she did not commit to introducing new age-based tiers or expanding the scheme to cover younger adults with chronic diseases. The Ministry stated it is actively tracking disease prevalence trends across different age groups to inform future adjustments to the Flexi-MediSave scheme and CDMP eligibility criteria. No specific timeline for potential policy changes or new screening data releases was provided in the response.

results.title: Tracking Outcomes and Equity of Government-funded Social Service Programmes and Impacts on Policy Modifications
results.summary: The Ministry of Social and Family Development (MSF) employs macro-level analysis of population trends and programme-level key performance indicators to monitor outcomes and equity. Rigorous evaluations, such as the 2024 study on KidSTART conducted with the Centre for Evidence and Implementation, confirmed improved child development and parent-child interactions, directly informing the decision to scale KidSTART nationwide and refine related programmes. Additionally, the National Council of Social Service (NCSS) launched the Sector Evaluation Framework in 2021 to standardize outcome measurement across the sector, recently publishing a Youth Development Outcomes Report that highlighted the importance of programme fidelity. These mechanisms ensure that evidence from assessments actively drives policy modifications and service delivery improvements.

results.title: Unauthorised Use of CDA Withdrawals at Optical Shops and Retail Pharmacies for Past Five Years
results.summary: Over the past five years, MSF identified approximately 1,600 unauthorised CDA transactions at optical shops and retail pharmacies, seeking refunds for affected children's accounts and reporting egregious cases to CAD. Due to high misuse risks and low CDA usage, MSF will discontinue CDA usage at these establishments from 1 January 2026. Parents may utilise alternative Government support schemes like the Baby Bonus Cash Gift and Child LifeSG Credits for purchases at these locations. Lower-income families requiring financial assistance can approach Social Service Offices. The Ministry will not implement new point-of-sales safeguards but will instead phase out usage entirely to prevent future misuse.

results.title: Underlying Causes of Death by Suicide among Adults Aged 30 to 39
results.summary: The Institute of Mental Health released a study in September identifying risk and protective factors for suicide, noting the issue is multi-factorial rather than single-cause. The Ministry of Health will continue collaborating with relevant agencies to improve prevention measures. Key interventions across all age groups include building mental resilience, spotting early risk indicators, encouraging timely help-seeking, and supporting individuals in crisis. These actions aim to enable timely support for at-risk individuals and potentially prevent suicides, particularly addressing the highest increase recorded in the 30 to 39 age group in 2024.

results.title: Update on Coastal Protection Studies on North and Western Part of Singapore
results.summary: Site-specific coastal protection studies for the north-west coast, covering coastal reservoirs and the shoreline from Lim Chu Kang to Woodlands, are underway with completion targeted for 2026. A south-west coast study dovetailing with the Jurong Island assessment will commence in 2026, with both projects aiming for completion by 2030. Preliminary and detailed designs by the Public Utilities Board and relevant agencies will follow study completion, with construction of recommended measures expected to start in the 2030s. Studies for the North and North-east coast remain pending.

results.title: Update on Implementation of Segregated Waste Collection
results.summary: The Government is progressively implementing segregated waste collection, having started with e-waste in 2021 and food waste from new large industrial and commercial buildings in 2024. Beverage container collection will extend to 2026. Public Waste Collectors are appointed via open tenders for six geographical sectors, open to all companies meeting experience and capacity requirements without a limit of three. The National Environment Agency selects successful tenderers based on merit to complement the National Recycling Programme.

results.title: Update on Progress and Completion of Review of Formula 1 Contracts
results.summary: The Deputy Prime Minister confirmed that the independent Review Committee has completed its assessment of the Singapore Grand Prix Term 4 Facilitation Agreement (2022–2028) and submitted its report. The Committee concluded that the Government's interests were sufficiently safeguarded, the deal represented a fair commercial exchange, and the economic benefits, including foreign visitorship and tourism receipts, exceeded targets. While the report remains unpublished due to business confidentiality, the Committee identified specific areas for process enhancement between the Singapore Tourism Board and Singapore GP Pte Ltd to further protect Government interests and improve future renewals. No irregularities were observed, and the Government's diligence in vetting the agreement through external legal counsel was affirmed.

results.title: Updated Nominal Basic Monthly Wages for Full-time Employed Residents by Deciles
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower released 2024 nominal basic monthly wages for full-time resident employees aged 15 and over, excluding employer CPF contributions, in Table 1. The Minister clarified that these figures represent only basic pay and do not include overtime, commissions, allowances, or bonuses. For a comprehensive view of total compensation, the Ministry recommends using gross wages, which incorporate all additional earnings components. Data is published regularly to reflect current wage outcomes across deciles.

results.title: Uptake of ComLink+ among Lower-income Families Not Living in Public Rental Flats
results.summary: As of end-September 2025, the Ministry of Social and Family Development provides ComLink+ support to approximately 1,000 lower-income families not residing in public rental flats. The Ministry aims to expand this support to an additional 2,000 families over the next two years. This initiative targets households outside the public rental flat system to enhance their digital connectivity. No further details on eligibility criteria or application processes were provided in the transcript.

results.title: Utilisation Rate and Cost of Marine Parade Cluster WeCare Shuttle Bus Service
results.summary: The Marine Parade Cluster WeCare Shuttle Service, a grassroots initiative launched in July 2024 with a $200,000 seed grant from the South East CDC, operated six routes during its pilot period. While utilisation rates varied by route and time, the Grassroots Organisations decided not to renew the service upon its contract expiry on 20 November 2025, opting instead to redirect resources toward more targeted community needs. Beyond the initial grant, all operational costs were covered by the GROs and donations. The Minister noted that future community shuttle services will depend on specific local needs, service costs, and the availability of alternatives, with further details to be shared directly by the Marine Parade Town Cluster GROs.

results.title: Utilisation Rates of NS LifeSG Credits and Considerations to Extend Validity Period
results.summary: As of end-September 2025, 87% of eligible past and present National Servicemen from the Singapore Armed Forces and Home Team had used their $200 NS LifeSG Credits disbursed in November 2024, with over 61% fully utilising them. The Ministry of Defence confirmed that the one-year validity period remains unchanged and aligns with previous voucher schemes, noting high utilisation rates. While direct SMS reminders and social media messages were broadcast to encourage redemption before expiry, the Minister did not announce an extension of the deadline or a review of unutilised credits. Consequently, the remaining 13% of recipients holding less than $2 in credits and those who have not used any credits face the existing expiry date without stated intervention.

results.title: Utilisation Rates of Overseas Markets Immersion Programme Funding by SMEs and Supporting Access to Funding
results.summary: The Overseas Markets Immersion Programme (OMIP), launched in 2024 with a $16 million two-year budget, has facilitated postings for over 70 locals, with more than 75% of participating companies being SMEs. The programme is open to both SME and non-SME entities to develop local workforce capabilities and support internationalisation. For companies requiring assistance, the Singapore Business Federation (SBF) acts as the Programme Partner, offering tailored guidance and conducting regular briefing sessions on application procedures. The Minister confirmed these support measures are in place to ensure effective SME access to the funding.

results.title: Verifying Efficacy of Protection Levels in Sunscreen Products
results.summary: Sunscreen products in Singapore are regulated as cosmetics under the Health Products Act by the Health Sciences Authority (HSA), requiring dealer notification rather than pre-market approval. HSA enforces compliance through risk-based post-market surveillance, sampling, and testing to verify declared UV filters and regulatory limits. While no pre-market approval is needed, suppliers face penalties of up to $50,000 in fines or two years' imprisonment for non-compliant products or misleading claims. HSA is currently investigating two notified products linked to Australian recalls and will align testing standards with international best practices to ensure accuracy.

results.title: Wage Targets for Lower-income Full-time Resident Workers and Compliance with National Wages Council's Recommendations by Sector and Profitability
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower confirmed that tripartite partners did not set specific wage targets for workers at the 5th, 10th, 15th, or 20th percentiles. However, real gross monthly income for full-time resident workers at the 20th percentile rose cumulatively by 6.3% from 2021 to 2024, exceeding the median worker's 3.2% increase. Compliance rates with the National Wages Council's lower-wage recommendations by sector and profitability are published annually in the Ministry's report on "Wage Practices for Lower-Wage Employees and Forward Expectations of Establishments." The 2021 Tripartite Workgroup on Lower-Wage Workers continues to guide efforts to narrow wage gaps.

results.title: Waiving Polyclinic Doctors' Memo Fees for SG Enable Schemes and Grants Applications
results.summary: Polyclinic doctors charge fees for medical memos certifying patient status for SG Enable schemes, as waiving these charges across the board contradicts the principle that services are already heavily subsidised. While patients requiring extra support can apply for additional financial assistance, scheme-managing agencies retain the discretion to fully cover specific charges. The Minister clarified that no blanket waiver exists, but alternative support mechanisms are available for eligible individuals.
